text: High-intensity aerobic interval training at 90-95% HRmax significantly improves VO2max more than long slow distance training at 70% HRmax or lactate threshold training at 85% HRmax, even when total work is matched.
4x4-minute intervals at 90-95% HRmax with 3-minute active recovery at 70% HRmax performed 3 days per week for 8 weeks increases VO2max by approximately 7.2%.
15/15 interval running (15s at 90-95% HRmax, 15s active rest at 70% HRmax) performed 3 days per week for 8 weeks increases VO2max by approximately 5.5%.
Long slow distance training at 70% HRmax and lactate threshold training at 85% HRmax do not significantly improve VO2max over 8 weeks in moderately trained individuals when performed 3 days per week.
High-intensity interval training at 90-95% HRmax increases cardiac stroke volume by approximately 10%, and changes in VO2max closely correspond with changes in stroke volume.
The 4x4-minute interval protocol produces a larger VO2max improvement (7.2%) than the 15/15 short-interval protocol (5.5%), though both are performed at the same intensity of 90-95% HRmax.
